creator: pgollucci@p6m7g8.com
text:
So I built 5 different combos of mod_perl2, apache{20,22}, and apr.

1)
APACHE_PORT is not defined
2)
APACHE_PORT=www/apache20
3)
APACHE_PORT=www/apache22
4)
APACHE_PORT=www/apache22
WITH_APR_FROM_PORTS=yes
NOPORTDOCS=yes
NOPORTEXAMPLES=yes
NOPORTDATA=yes
5)
APACHE_PORT=www/apache22
WITH_MYSQL=yes
WITH_PGSQL=yes
WITH_LDAP=yes
WITH_NDBM=yes
WITH_DBD=yes

http://tinderbox-amd64.p6m7g8.net/index.php?action=describe_port&id=695

None of these had issues.

Additionally, I'm pretty sure I've fixed this specifically before:
(see attached 2 files)

A) Can you add the output of:
$ cat /var/db/ports/apr/options
$ cat /var/db/ports/mod_perl2/options

B) If you have any releavant /etc/make.conf magic at play add that too.

C) Verify these match to confirm you have the latest without mods:
$ md5 www/mod_perl2/Makefile www/mod_perl2/pkg-plist devel/apr/Makefile
MD5 (www/mod_perl2/Makefile) = cbb5f1321c53f2a751c53509bb2d03fc
MD5 (www/mod_perl2/pkg-plist) = 882105ee7293431bb533fa36fc810e89
MD5 (devel/apr/Makefile) = 14bd8edb7aae7c0f8d38ff126f10c131

D) Please verify manually that that deinstall/pkg_deletes actually remove
at least /usr/local/bin/apr-1-config and /usr/local/inclue/apr-1/apr.h.

E) Please verify your httpd install _really_ isn't install its own apr.
    Now that I right this, I'll bet this is it.


--
